full_response:
We label the strategy profile by the pair  
(x_A , x_B) ∈ [0,1] × [0,1], where x_A (resp. x_B) is the fraction of the link Alice (resp. Bob) asks for.

Recall the utilities  

             u_A(x_A , x_B) = { 0                    if x_A + x_B > 1  
                                   x_A (1 − x_A − x_B)  otherwise

             u_B(x_A , x_B) = { 0                    if x_A + x_B > 1  
                                   x_B (1 − x_A − x_B)  otherwise

Goal:  show that (x_A , x_B) = (1/3 , 1/3) is a Nash equilibrium and compute u_A + u_B at that point.

--------------------------------------------------------------------
Step 1:  Best-response analysis for Alice when Bob fixes x_B = 1/3
--------------------------------------------------------------------
For any x_A ∈ [0,1]:

• If x_A + 1/3 > 1  ⇔  x_A > 2/3, then u_A = 0.

• Otherwise 0 ≤ x_A ≤ 2/3 and  

        u_A(x_A , 1/3) = x_A (1 − x_A − 1/3)  
                        = x_A (2/3 − x_A)  
                        = −x_A^2 + (2/3) x_A.

This is a concave quadratic (coefficient of x_A^2 is −1).  
Take derivative and set to 0:

         d/dx_A [ −x_A^2 + (2/3)x_A ] = −2x_A + 2/3 = 0  
         ⇒ x_A = 1/3.

Second derivative −2 < 0 confirms a maximum.  
Value at the maximum: u_A(1/3 , 1/3) = (1/3)(1/3) = 1/9.

For any other x_A ∈ [0,2/3] the value is smaller (strictly smaller unless x_A = 1/3), and for x_A > 2/3 the value is 0.  
Hence Alice’s unique best response to x_B = 1/3 is x_A = 1/3.

--------------------------------------------------------------------
Step 2:  Symmetric argument for Bob
--------------------------------------------------------------------
Because the utilities are symmetric in the players, replacing A by B gives:

When Alice plays x_A = 1/3, Bob’s best response maximises  
u_B(1/3 , x_B) = x_B (2/3 − x_B),  0 ≤ x_B ≤ 2/3,  
and the same calculation shows the maximiser is x_B = 1/3.

--------------------------------------------------------------------
Step 3:  Nash equilibrium conclusion
--------------------------------------------------------------------
At (x_A , x_B) = (1/3 , 1/3) each player is playing a best response to the other; hence no unilateral deviation can improve a player’s utility. Thus (1/3 , 1/3) is a Nash equilibrium.

--------------------------------------------------------------------
Step 4:  Total utility at the equilibrium
--------------------------------------------------------------------
u_A(1/3 , 1/3) = 1/9,   u_B(1/3 , 1/3) = 1/9  
⇒ u_A + u_B = 1/9 + 1/9 = 2/9.

\boxed{\text{The profile }(x_A,x_B)=(1/3,1/3)\text{ is a Nash equilibrium, and it yields total utility }u_A+u_B=\;2/9.}

Confidence: 0.26
extracted_answer: \text{The profile }(x_A,x_B)=(1/3,1/3)\text{ is a Nash equilibrium, and it yields total utility }u_A+u_B=\;2/9.
